steps.title: Use the domain before squaring
steps.content:
Because the left side is a square root, the right side must be nonnegative: $28-x \ge 0$. Let $t=28-x$, so $t \ge 0$ and $x=28-t$. The equation becomes
$$\sqrt{k-(28-t)}=t$$
Now both sides are nonnegative, so squaring preserves the solution condition:
$$k-28+t=t^2$$
$$k=t^2-t+28$$

steps.title: Find the minimum k that gives one solution
steps.content:
Now the number of solutions is the number of nonnegative $t$-values satisfying $k=t^2-t+28$. Complete the square:
$$k=t^2-t+28=\left(t-\frac12\right)^2+\frac{111}{4}$$
The minimum possible value of $k$ is $\frac{111}{4}$, and it occurs at one value, $t=\frac12$. Therefore:
$$4k=4\left(\frac{111}{4}\right)=111$$
The answer is 111.
